task: Instruction: Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6549951. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.

task: Instruction: Verify that patient S6415739 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order. If there are zero orders, create one. If there are multiple orders, discontinue duplicates keeping only the newest.

[P1] If a search returns no results, broaden to alternative coding systems or name-based search before finalizing absence.

task: Instruction: Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1374652 has a matching naloxone prescription. If an opioid order is active without naloxone, create a naloxone order.
episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified an active opioid order but then searched for naloxone using a text-based query instead of a standard code system (e.g., RxNorm). The action was invalid because the query may miss coded naloxone orders.
failure_analysis: Agent used text search for naloxone instead of coding system, risking missed matches and invalid action.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for medications, always use standard coding systems (e.g., RxNorm) not free-text names to ensure complete coverage.
[P1] For drug pairing workflows, after identifying the primary drug class orders, search for the secondary drug class using its standard therapeutic class code.
[P1] If a code-based search fails, fall back to name search only after exhausting coding system options.

task: Instruction: Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S1374652. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.
episodic_summary: Agent was asked to check COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if last dose >12 months ago. No vaccine records found. Agent incorrectly ordered a booster without first establishing a primary series exists, violating workflow logic.
failure_analysis: Agent failed because it jumped to ordering a booster when no prior vaccination records existed, instead of recognizing that a primary series must be established before a booster can be given.
action_guidelines: [P1] When no prior vaccination records exist for a vaccine requiring a primary series, do not order a booster; instead, initiate the primary series or flag the case for clinical review.
[P1] Before ordering a booster dose, verify that at least one prior dose of the vaccine exists in the patient's record.
[P1] When searching for vaccination records, use standard vaccine coding systems (CVX, CPT) and search by both code and vaccine group name to ensure complete retrieval.

task: Instruction: Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S3032536. If the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'.
episodic_summary: Agent found CT Abdomen on 2023-11-09, but current time was 2023-11-07. Incorrectly concluded procedure was in the future, ignoring that a future date cannot be a performed procedure. Failed to re-query for correct most recent date.
failure_analysis: Agent accepted a future date as valid procedure date without verifying plausibility. Should have recognized inconsistency and searched again or flagged data error.
action_guidelines: [P1] If a procedure date is later than the current time, flag it as invalid and re-query for the actual most recent past procedure date.
[P1] When comparing dates, always confirm the procedure date is in the past relative to the current time before applying age thresholds.
[P1] Do not accept a single search result as final if its date is implausible (e.g., future); broaden search or re-check query parameters.

task: Instruction: Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S6530532 has a matching naloxone prescription. If an opioid order is active without naloxone, create a naloxone order.

failure_analysis: Agent searched for specific opioid drug names rather than the therapeutic class, missing active orders. Should have queried by drug class to capture all formulations and coding variants.
action_guidelines: [P1] When verifying active orders for a drug class, always search by the therapeutic class name first, not individual drug names, to capture all formulations and coding variants.
[P1] If searching by specific drug names yields no results, broaden the search using the therapeutic class before concluding absence.

task: Instruction: Retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for patient S1579803. Based on the results, take appropriate action according to the protocol.

task: Instruction: Check if patient S6538722 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.

task: Instruction: Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S6474456. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.
episodic_summary: Agent checked last influenza vaccine date for patient S6474456, found it was 124 days ago, concluded no new order needed. However, the instruction required ordering if >365 days; the agent misapplied the condition, incorrectly stating it was less than 365 days when it was actually less.
failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly evaluated the date difference: the last vaccine was on 2023-09-07 and current date was 2024-01-09, which is only ~124 days, not more than 365. The agent should have recognized this as less than 365 and correctly concluded no order needed, but the reasoning was flawed and the final answer was wrong due to misstatement.
action_guidelines: [P1] When calculating date differences, explicitly compute the number of days between two dates using a reliable method before applying conditional logic.
[P1] Double-check the condition: if the instruction says 'if more than 365 days ago, order', ensure you compare the actual elapsed days, not a reversed or misread comparison.
[P1] Before finishing, review the numeric result and the logical conclusion to ensure the condition was applied correctly.

task: Instruction: Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S6550627 has a matching naloxone prescription. If an opioid order is active without naloxone, create a naloxone order.
episodic_summary: Agent incorrectly reported no active opioid orders, failing to verify across all relevant resources and statuses. The patient had active opioid orders that were missed, leading to a false negative and missing naloxone orders.
failure_analysis: Agent failed because it did not search comprehensively for active opioid orders across all relevant resource types (MedicationRequest, ServiceRequest, Procedure) and statuses (active, draft, completed). It prematurely concluded absence without thorough verification.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before concluding no active orders exist, search across all relevant resource types (MedicationRequest, ServiceRequest, Procedure, Observation) and all possible statuses (active, draft, completed, entered-in-error).
[P1] When verifying medication pairings, retrieve all active orders for the primary drug class first, then cross-check each against the secondary drug class.
[P1] If a search returns no results, re-verify by broadening the search to include alternative coding systems or name-based queries before concluding absence.

task: Instruction: Verify that patient S2823623 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order. If there are zero orders, create one. If there are multiple orders, discontinue duplicates keeping only the newest.
episodic_summary: Agent verified DVT prophylaxis order count for a patient by checking only heparin orders, missing other anticoagulant classes. It found one heparin order and concluded no action needed, but failed to search across all relevant drug classes (LMWH, DOAC) for active orders.
failure_analysis: Agent failed because it did not search across all drug classes that constitute DVT prophylaxis (heparin, LMWH, DOACs, etc.). It only checked for heparin, missing other active orders and leading to an incorrect order count.
action_guidelines: [P1] When verifying active orders for a therapeutic category, search across all drug classes within that category (e.g., for DVT prophylaxis: heparin, LMWH, DOAC, warfarin) before concluding the count.
[P1] Query by therapeutic class or category name, not individual drug names, to capture all formulations and coding variants.
[P1] Cross-check retrieved orders against the full list of relevant drug classes for the given protocol.
[P1] If the instruction specifies a therapeutic category, expand the search to include all drugs that fulfill that category.

task: Instruction: Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S0658561. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.

action_guidelines: [P1] When no records are found for a requested item, report absence; do not infer time-based conditions from missing data.
[P1] Do not create a clinical order unless all preconditions are explicitly satisfied by existing data.
[P1] If a required search returns zero results, stop and report the finding rather than proceeding to action.

task: Instruction: Retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for patient S0547588. Based on the results, take appropriate action according to the protocol.

failure_analysis: Agent succeeded because it retrieved all available lab results, selected the most recent by date, explicitly documented the absence of FT4, and applied protocol thresholds before deciding on action.

task: Instruction: Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S6352985.

[P1] If initial query returns no data for the target observation type, verify the observation code is correct and retry with corrected parameters before concluding absence.

task: Instruction: Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S3032536.

task: Instruction: Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S3032536. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.

task: Instruction: Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S1777769. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.

task: Instruction: Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S6500497 has a matching naloxone prescription. If an opioid order is active without naloxone, create a naloxone order.

task: Instruction: Check if patient S6539215 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.
episodic_summary: Agent checked catheter placement duration but failed to verify removal orders before proceeding. Attempted to create a removal request without confirming absence of removal orders, leading to invalid action.
failure_analysis: Agent did not thoroughly search for existing removal orders (e.g., by procedure or observation) before initiating a new service request. The workflow requires confirming no active removal order exists first.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before creating a removal request for a medical device, first search for any active removal orders or procedures using relevant codes (e.g., SNOMED for removal) and verify no removal is already scheduled.

[P1] When evaluating duration of device placement, confirm the current time and placement time using standardized datetime formats, and ensure the threshold (e.g., 48 hours) is exceeded before proceeding.
[P1] If a search for removal orders returns no results, explicitly document the absence before creating a new order.
[P1] Do not proceed to create a service request until all prerequisite checks (e.g., no existing removal order, device still in place) are completed and confirmed.

task: Instruction: Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2111822. If the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'.
episodic_summary: Agent searched for a CT Abdomen but used non-standard local codes and prematurely concluded that a CT Chest Abdomen Pelvis did not qualify. It failed to retrieve all relevant procedures first and did not compare dates properly before deciding on ordering.
failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly filtered out a relevant CT procedure based on local code mismatch instead of evaluating by body region and date. It should have first retrieved all CT procedures covering the abdomen, then applied the 12-month threshold.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a specific procedure type (e.g., CT Abdomen), retrieve all procedures that include that body region regardless of local code; use broad text or body site searches first.
[P1] Do not discard a procedure solely because its local code does not match an expected list; assess clinical relevance by description or body site.
[P1] After retrieving all candidate procedures, list their dates explicitly and select the most recent before applying any time threshold.
[P1] If the most recent relevant procedure is less than 12 months old, do not proceed to order a new one.

task: Instruction: Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6488980. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.
episodic_summary: Agent searched for COVID-19 vaccination records and found none, then terminated without ordering a booster. The correct action should have been to order an initial COVID-19 vaccine series since no prior doses exist, not just stop.
failure_analysis: Agent misinterpreted 'booster' as requiring a prior dose, but the instruction was to review vaccination status and order a booster if last dose >12 months ago. With no prior doses, the correct response is to order the primary series, not conclude no action.
action_guidelines: [P1] If no prior doses exist for a vaccine series, do not skip; instead initiate the primary vaccination series as the first dose.
[P1] When reviewing vaccination status, distinguish between 'booster' (requires prior doses) and 'initial dose' (no prior doses required) and act accordingly.
[P1] If a patient has zero records for a vaccine, the appropriate action is to start the series, not terminate the workflow.

task: Instruction: Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S6540602. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.

task: Instruction: Retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for patient S1521703. Based on the results, take appropriate action according to the protocol.

task: Instruction: Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S0547588. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.

task: Instruction: Verify that patient S6307599 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order. If there are zero orders, create one. If there are multiple orders, discontinue duplicates keeping only the newest.

action_guidelines: [P1] When verifying active orders for a drug class, always search by the therapeutic class name first, not individual drug names, to capture all formulations and coding variants.
[P1] If initial search returns no results, paginate to check remaining pages before concluding absence.
[P1] Do not rely on individual drug name searches alone for class-level verification.

task: Instruction: Retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for patient S6426560. Based on the results, take appropriate action according to the protocol.

task: Instruction: Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S6268253.

action_guidelines: [P1] When asked for vital sign averages, first retrieve all observations for the patient using the vital-signs category and a broad date range covering the maximum window.
[P1] If a specific LOINC code search returns no results, verify the code is valid for that patient before broadening search.
[P1] Always confirm data existence with a wide query before narrowing by specific codes or time windows.

task: Instruction: Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S2450227.

action_guidelines: [P1] When calculating averages over time windows, always retrieve the patient's relevant observations (e.g., heart rate) with timestamps for the full required range first, then compute each mean from actual data.
[P1] If no observations exist for a time window, return an explicit 'No data' indicator instead of a fabricated numeric value.

task: Instruction: Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S1374652.

action_guidelines: [P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately.

[P1] Before concluding data absence, search with broad parameters (no date filter) to ensure all records are captured.

action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a drug, use therapeutic class names and standard coding systems (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) to capture all formulations and coding variants.
[P1] Before creating a new order, re-verify that no active orders exist for the same drug class using multiple search strategies.
[P1] If a search returns zero results, broaden the search by using alternative terminologies or class-level queries before concluding absence.
[P1] When creating a medication order, ensure all required fields (e.g., medication reference, dosage, route, patient reference) are present and valid.

task: Instruction: Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S6500497.
episodic_summary: Agent attempted to retrieve heart rate observations but received blood pressure data. It correctly identified the need for a specific LOINC code but made an invalid API call, ending the episode.

action_guidelines: [P1] Before making an API call, verify the endpoint URL, parameters, and encoding to ensure validity.
[P1] If initial query returns wrong data type, check the coding system and code used; retry with correct code before concluding.

task: Instruction: Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6551923. Find the most recent COVID-19 vaccine and if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, order a COVID booster.
episodic_summary: Agent attempted to find COVID-19 vaccination status by querying MedicationRequest with patient ID and category, but failed to complete the task within limits. No vaccination-specific search (e.g., vaccine codes or Immunization resource) was used.
failure_analysis: Agent used MedicationRequest with a generic category filter instead of querying Immunization records or using vaccine-specific codes (e.g., CVX) to find COVID-19 vaccination status.
action_guidelines: [P1] To check vaccination status, query the Immunization resource with patient ID and vaccine code (e.g., CVX for COVID-19), not MedicationRequest.
